reasoning: Estimated baseline probabilities using empirical climatology data for Saint-Girons. Mean average daily temperatures decline robustly through autumn and winter: ~12.5°C for late October, ~11.4°C for early November, ~7.5°C for late November, and ~6.0°C for late January. Treating temperatures on the base date and future dates as mostly independent random variables, the expectation of the difference (future - baseline) drops from roughly -1.5°C to -6.5°C. Comparing this to the combined specific daily variance (~4°C-5°C per day difference), the physical probability of future individual days being warmer than October 26th drops cleanly and substantially below 50%, with probabilities estimated around 38%, 19%, and 14%. While generalized uncertainty increases across time, the dominant deterministic seasonal temperature forcing strictly reduces the likelihood of the event.
